topicName: Word Problems on Addition and Subtraction
description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for word problems on addition and subtraction

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has strong computation and problem-solving basics, can focus on application with challenge

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of word problem types with real-world application

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in computation or problem-solving basics, needs targeted review

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant review of computation and problem-solving basics before word problems

videoSequence.reason: Gentle intro to one-step addition problems

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you're ready to continue with word problems.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

addition_subtraction_mastery.conceptName: Mastery of addition and subtraction (with and without regrouping)

identifying_operations.conceptName: Identifying which operation to use (addition vs subtraction)

reading_comprehension.conceptName: Understanding word problems and extracting information

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: Maya had 3,456 stickers. She gave 1,289 to her friend. How many does she have left?
A shop sold 2,345 books in January and 3,456 books in February. How many books total?

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You understand one-step word problems well.

questions.question: A farmer had 5,000 kg rice. He sold 2,000 kg and then harvested 3,000 kg more. How much rice does he have now?
questions.explanation: Step 1: After selling → 5,000 - 2,000 = 3,000 kg. Step 2: After harvesting → 3,000 + 3,000 = 6,000 kg.

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for complex multi-step problems

ifWrong.message: Practice more two-step problems before continuing

skip_ahead.action: Skip mixed practice, jump to challenge

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on keywords and problem-solving steps

suggest_break.trigger: After 3 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend solving 10-15 word problems with parent/teacher

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You have strong computation and problem-solving skills. You're ready to master word problems. We've created a fast-track playlist with challenge content.

good_score.message: Good work! You understand basics well. Let's build your word problem-solving skills with complete coverage.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in computation or problem-solving basics. Don't worry! We'll start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need.

low_score.message: You need to review addition/subtraction and problem-solving basics first. We've created a playlist focused on building strong foundation.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how word problem skills build on each other

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 75% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

problemSolvingAccuracy.description: % of students who can correctly solve word problems

operationSelection.description: % of students who correctly identify which operation to use

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 36-40 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 38-42 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 52-58 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (37 min) + retake + main content (26 min) = 63-72 total minutes, expected 65-80% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.solution1: Emphasize keywords: 'total', 'altogether' → add; 'left', 'difference', 'more/less' → subtract. Practice identifying keywords first
commonChallenges.solution2: Teach: Read twice, underline important information (given, asked). What do I know? What do I need to find?
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students making computation errors even with correct operation
commonChallenges.solution3: Remind: Solve carefully with regrouping. Check answer using opposite operation or estimation
commonChallenges.solution4: Teach: Break into steps. Solve first step, use answer in second step. Label intermediate results clearly
